entry.title: Generative AI boosting Process Automation? Come see our the results at SAP TechEd!
entry.content:
Where do we come from in Process Automation?

In a blog post I published 9 months ago, I told you the story of the Process Automation solution at SAP, from SAP Intelligent RPA to SAP Build Process Automation.

Recently, Thomas Volmering explained how we are taking a big step forward by bringing Generative AI capabilities to process automation. In fact, we've made great progress this year by infusing GenAI into the 3 pillars that make up our Enterprise Automation suite: SAP Signavio, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Integration Suite.

I'm excited to tell you that you'll see the results of our efforts in a month's time at SAP TechEd.

Just for fun, I asked NightCafe Studio to create "a photorealistic image showing a crowd of software developers from all over the world. They are at SAP TechEd conference, attending a presentation on generative AI in Enterprise Automation solutions".  ➡️

What do you think of this image?

Tell me in the comments.

 

 

Here we are: Generative AI across Process Automation!

I invite you to register for our online event, and to watch the Launch Keynote – “Where ideas get real”: you will see and amazing end-to-end demo based on a real-life use case… but I for sure won’t spoil it. 😉

After that, I highly recommend watching Thomas Volmering and Alexander Rother from SAP, with Björn Schulz, from  REWE digital in session AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions.

They will present you a new approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, you can smoothly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs. Learn from experts about best practices, innovative solutions, and practical applications that can improve your development processes and drive business growth.

In addition, do not miss to add these sessions to your agenda:

XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio

Presented by Stephan Schluchter and Robin Kiwitt, this session will let you discover how enterprise automation combines the power of SAP Build solutions and SAP Integration Suite running on SAP BTP with SAP Signavio solutions. Explore how you can act based on process insights and rapidly transition from process modeling and simulation to executable actions. Learn how to achieve process optimization by leveraging the capability of BPMN models imported into SAP Build, and act on top of the process mining and modeling in SAP Signavio.

AD109 | Experience the next level of Enterprise Automation with SAP 

In this session presented by Palak Garg and Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan you will explore the latest enhancements using SAP Build solutions, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio solutions together.

Our experts will show you how to drive your AI-powered business transformation by combining process analysis, process automation, and integration capabilities. They will show you how to extend your approval processes with API-based integrations, and how you can leverage event-driven architectures, turn process insights and modeling outcomes into actions, and orchestrate your processes at scale across heterogenous landscapes.

AD110 | Accelerate development with the latest AI capabilities in SAP Build 

In this session with Marc Huber and Rupam Bhattacharya you will discover the power of Joule in SAP Build solutions to accelerate your development process and the AI features in SAP Build that improve user productivity.

Dive into the latest AI features designed for developers, enabling you to extend SAP S/4 HANA. Experience first-hand Gen AI-powered fusion development in SAP Build. Learn how to productively automate processes and workflow with AI capabilities. Get an overview of what's new and the latest in AI capabilities in SAP Build.

IN105 | Unleash the power of AI-driven enterprise automation with SAP solutions

With Palak Garg and Evgeniy Gorbunov, learn how to extend and integrate your business processes by combining SAP Integration Suite with SAP Build and SAP Signavio solutions, supported by AI capabilities and prebuilt content.

Future-proof your journey to become an intelligent enterprise using automation-based integration. Achieve the next level of integration efficiency and process improvement and drive innovation by infusing AI capabilities at scale, all while supporting it with the rich set of prebuilt content.

 

Join us at SAP TechEd 2024!

So if you're a process automation enthusiast, you now have your agenda to get the most out of SAP TechEd 2024. See you next month! 👍🏻 

➡️ Register now for SAP TechEd 2024 ✅

➡️ Join our Enterprise Automation initiative ✅

entry.title: Got security? Tune in to our sessions at SAP TechEd 2024!
entry.content:
If you want to find out more about the security services available with SAP BTP, you've come to the right place. Let me point you to the three sessions you should attend for a concise summary of the most important SAP BTP security features and strategies. 

Start off with XP103 | Got security? Services for security and compliance with SAP BTP and hear from Michael Friedrich, Chief Product Owner for SAP BTP security, and Nathalie Bergstroem, SAP BTP compliance developer, which security services you should check out. You will learn about the key principles for developing robust applications that comply with industry regulations, using secure development practices and compliance services. Nathalie will take you on a deep dive into the SAP Data Privacy Integration service, as well as our SAP Audit Log service architecture.

Next up are two sessions that focus on identity and access management. For the first one, we've teamed up with our partner Microsoft. Martin Raepple, technical program manager at Microsoft, and I will explain how your hybrid environments can benefit from our collaboration in XP202 | Identity lifecycle management with SAP BTP and Microsoft Entra. We will show you how to combine SAP Cloud Identity Services with Microsoft Entra, a universal identity platform, to create a holistic user lifecycle management for both SAP and non-SAP solutions, in the cloud and on premise.

The last session is a special treat, brought to you right from the people who know our customer implementations best. Two of our expert security consultants, Oxana Junkereit and Tobias Lejczyk, will share some of their extensive expertise in XP203 | SAP Cloud Identity Services: Best practices for a central user management. Learn how to get started, how to benefit from SAP Cloud Identity Services, and explore the concepts and capabilities of these core SAP BTP services. 

Check out all the other sessions SAP TechEd has to offer in our session catalog, and make sure you read the Real Talk with Stephanie and Konstantin | SAP Business Technology Platform Track Leads for some background and recommendations on our other track sessions!

entry.title: Explore SAP Build Low-Code at TechEd 2024
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is right across the corner and the spotlight shines brighter than ever on SAP Build—a comprehensive suite of low-code and pro-code tools designed to accelerate application development and automation with the power of generative AI. Whether you're an experienced developer or just beginning your digital journey, SAP Build is here to provide business you with the tools needed to simplify app creation, automate processes, and design digital workspaces.

This blog will focus on SAP Build’s low-code offerings at TechEd 2024, highlighting key sessions that showcase how low-code can empower you to drive innovation and transformation within your organization.

SAP TechEd Experience 2024 

SAP TechEd 2024 kicks off with an exciting, free virtual event that’s open to everyone, offering a truly engaging experience no matter where you are. It is a great chance to connect with industry experts, developers, and IT leaders to discover innovative strategies that will elevate your career and drive your company’s success.

   📅 Save the date: October 8–9, 2024 

   🤝 Interactive, accessible, and virtual event

   🚀 Register now! 

Following this, SAP TechEd on Tour will bring deep dive, in-person learning experience to various locations worldwide. 

 

Highlight: SAP Build Low-Code at TechEd virtual 2024 

   🔷 AD100 | Unlock SAP Build and ABAP Cloud interoperability to elevate your extensions

Speakers: Thomas Volmering, Björn Schulz, Alexander Rother 

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 6:00 PM - 6:45 PM CEST

Explore a fresh approach to building extensions while keeping your core clean. Learn how SAP Build solutions and the ABAP Cloud development model work together to foster fusion development. Whether you prefer pro-code or low-code tools, you can smoothly integrate them to build extensions tailored to your needs.

   🔷 AD102 | Unlock SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility with SAP Build

Speakers: Florian Buech, Karthik Thirunavukkarasan

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 11:00 AM - 11:25 AM CEST

Explore the latest capabilities in SAP Build solutions to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping your core clean. The extensibility wizard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides direct access to various extension options both on stack and side by side on SAP Business Technology Platform. New remote function call connectivity in SAP Build enables clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using tier-two BAPIs.

   🔷 AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build

Speakers: Tim Back, Anne Keller

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 7:00 PM - 7:25 PM CEST

Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.

   🔷 AD108 | Enhance your business efficiency with SAP Build

Speakers: Yumnah Abbasi, Sruthi Chiramel

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:30 PM - 8:55 PM CEST

Find out how customers extend their processes with SAP Build low-code solutions across HR, procurement, supply chain, finance, and sustainability applications. Learn how you can use prebuilt content in SAP Build to accelerate and benefit from the latest features, such as guided experiences.

   🔷 AD109 | Experience the next level of enterprise automation with SAP

Speakers: Seshadri Sreenivas Ramanarayanan, Palak Garg

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 1:00 PM - 1:25 PM CEST

Explore the latest enhancements using SAP Build solutions, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio solutions together. Drive your AI-powered business transformation by combining process analysis, process automation, and integration capabilities. Extend your approval processes with API-based integrations, and find out how you can leverage event-driven architectures, turn process insights and modelling outcomes into actions, and orchestrate your processes at scale in heterogenous landscapes.

   🔷 AD110 | Accelerate development with the latest AI capabilities in SAP Build

Speakers: Rupam Bhattacharya, Marc Huber

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:00 PM - 8:25 PM CEST

Discover the power of Joule in SAP Build solutions to accelerate your development process and AI features in SAP build that improve productivity of its users. Dive into the latest AI features designed for developers, enabling you to extend SAP S/4 HANA. Experience Gen AI powered fusion development firsthand in SAP Build. Learn how to automate processes and work flow productively with AI capabilities. Get a glimpse of what’s new and latest in AI features within SAP Build.

   🔷 AD181 | Develop applications and processes with SAP Build and Joule (Jump-Start session) 

Speakers: Eric Solberg, Patrick Schad

🗓 9th Oct 2024  ⏰ 9:00 PM - 9:25 PM CEST

Immerse yourself in creating comprehensive intelligent applications using SAP Build solutions in this hands-on session. Gain comprehensive knowledge of full-stack development, process automation, and business site creation with SAP Build. Learn how SAP Cloud Application Programming Model is leveraged in a highly productive development environment with Joule technology.

   🔷 AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills

Speakers: Maria Kondratyeva, Miguel Angel Meza Martinez

🗓 8th Oct 2024  ⏰ 8:00 PM - 8:25 PM CEST

Discover the powerful synergy between Joule technology and SAP Build solutions that facilitates a smooth extension-building process. Learn how to enhance AI-powered capabilities in Joule with custom-developed enterprise skills tailored to your company's specific needs. By integrating with both SAP and third-party applications, Joule can instantly apply its newfound knowledge within SAP systems. 

----------------------------------------

In addition to SAP Build highlights, SAP TechEd virtual offers a wide range of sessions, organized into six content tracks. These tracks cover the latest SAP technologies, products, and solutions, ensuring that you can dive deep into the topics most relevant to your role and industry.

   🔍 Browse the full session catalog!

In the Application Development and Automation track, you can dive into the latest innovations in SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Code, SAPUI5, Enterprise Automation with SAP, and more. The track offers practical demos and insights from our top speakers and product experts. 

   👩‍💻 Check out this blogpost for further details!

----------------------------------------

I hope this sneak peek has sparked your excitement to be part of SAP TechEd 2024 and explore all the latest updates around SAP Build and beyond. 

We can’t wait to see you there – Happy TechEd! 😊

entry.title: SAP Developer News September 12th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/12/sap-developer-news-september-12th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP CodeJam – A look back at recent events in India

  • CodeJam Group: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/gh-p/code-jam
  • CodeJam Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/eb-p/codejam-events
  • CodeJam topic list: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407

SAP Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd Watch Party

  • Devtoberfest Group Page: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • SAP TechEd Virtual:  https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

SAP TechEd Three Reasons to Attend

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP TechEd Session Catalog: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog

Devtoberfest – One Week to Go!

  • Devtoberfest 2024 Contest https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-contest/ba-p/13781593
  • Devtoberfest Group https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 SAP CodeJam
1:07 SAP Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd Watch Party
2:36 SAP TechEd – 3 Reasons
3:38 Devtoberfest

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 12, 2024.

[Josh] Hey everybody, I know that you're excited for SAP's Devtoberfest and TechEd. These two events are happening. Devtoberfest starts in two weeks and then TechEd is going to be October 8th and 9th. Now what's cool about this is while TechEd is being filmed by all the developer advocates and SAP experts from Germany, we're beaming it out live over the internet and you can watch it. How about with Devtoberfest sessions, you know when they're going to be happening, you and your friends can get together and you can actually have a watch party. You can do this for Devtoberfest, and you can do it for TechEd. So I offer up, if you happen to be near where I am, like you guys know I love being on stage, well I'm on a stage right now. And then out here, we've got a lovely amount of seating. We could have a watch party here in my local hometown. Just kidding. It would be probably hard to fill all of those seats because you're all working in different locations. You're all in your own offices, or maybe you're going to get together in a local restaurant. But hey, if you do want to get together, tell us about it. Give us a note, let us know that you're meeting and having a watch party and we'll promote it online and the developer advocates might even be able to talk about it or connect with you live from TechEd. So hey, if you're having a watch party, if you're watching any of the Devtoberfest sessions or if you're going to be watching any of the SAP TechEd sessions, let us know that you're having a watch party and just like my friend Luis who's down in Brazil, he told me he's trying to really get a lot of people energized to attend a lot of the Devtoberfest sessions and participate. Let us know that you're getting together and doing that if you're in a group and we'll give you a shout out. See you later, bye.

[Tom] The excitement is building as we approach this year's TechEd virtual. Let me give you a few of my top reasons to attend our live two-day community building deep dive learning program. Number one, 90 live sessions. That's right, we're talking live code, demos, lectures, and deep dive jumpstart sessions. Number two, live chat and QA. Have a question for one of our session presenters? Want to connect with an attendee with similar interest? You could do both within the chat functionality built right into the SAP TechEd platform. Number three, deconstructing the announcements. The SAP Developer Advocates will be your guides throughout the entire two-day show. We'll be there to break down the keynote announcements and deconstruct the latest innovations driving the digital transformation. Sign up now and explore your opportunities to build your expertise, sharpen your skills, and broaden your community. See you online in October.

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here. I hope everyone had a safe and happy summer, and that you have all made it back from vacation in one piece. Because today I want to remind you that it's time to get back to work. And to help you all settle in a bit and get comfortable, yes that's right folks, it's time for Devtoberfest, and it all starts in less than two weeks from now. Everything that we've all loved from previous years is back. A ton of sessions on the latest technologies coming out of our labs. We have tutorials which you can do to earn even more points and of course our game board which shows the progress towards Nirvana. We have sessions across all of our core technology topics like ABAP and CAP, Front End, Integration, learning, AI and data and finally tooling. All of which will stream on the SAP developers YouTube channel. So there is a ton of learning opportunities for you at Devtoberfest and let's not forget the grand prize. The grand prize winners will receive a nice trip to next year's SAP TechEd event. So if you haven't registered for Devtoberfest yet do it right now. No seriously stop watching the news and go register but before you do DJ has one more call to action for you over to you DJ.

[DJ] Yeah thanks Rich and we want to hear from you. What are you most looking forward to with  Devtoberfest? Is it the points? Is it the potential prizes? Is it moving up that game board? Or is it, as it is with me, the prospect of learning cool new stuff? We want to know, so let us know in the comments and see you at Devtoberfest!

entry.title: Real Talk with Britta & Eduardo | Data & Analytics Track Leads
entry.content:
Hello everybody! I’m Eduardo, a Product Manager in Data & Analytics, with a focus on SAP  Data Intelligence and SAP Datasphere. I was born in Brazil and love travelling the world with my family, meet people, discover new cultures and different types of food.

Hi guys, I’m Britta, a Product Manager for Data & Analytics. I live in “Pfalz”, a nice region in Germany, close to Walldorf, where we enjoy delicious white wine. When I’m not dealing with SAP, I love playing the violin and hang out with my kids.

“Data is at the heart of the new, sustainable enterprise!”

Yes, indeed it is. Data & Analytics gives organizations a competitive advantage, enabling informed decisions and uncovering new opportunities. Especially when you think of how powerful AI has become, opportunities are infinite. However, the best AI models are only as strong as its database. Poor data leads to flawed predictions and underperforming AIs.

Our track provides a set of hot topics and new features along our closely integrated product portfolio to help you to stay in control of your data:

  1. SAP Datasphere serves as SAP’s Business Data Fabric solution: Deliver trusted, context-rich data to every user with business context​ and logic intact​.
  2. Build Intelligent Data Applications with SAP HANA​: Utilize generative AI, context-awareness,​ and connected to vital business data.
  3. Extended Planning and Analysis​ with SAP Analytics Cloud: Elevate enterprise planning with an optimized, vertically integrated consumption layer for planning and analytics workloads.
  4. Improve your business-critical information with Master Data Governance.

What inspired you to specialize in this track?

With this portfolio we empower organizations to make more informed, efficient, and strategic decisions. We are also very excited to collaborate with so many outstanding SAP experts and customers. This is what inspired us to specialize in this track.

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit?

In Data & Analytics, we turn abstract concepts and creative thoughts into tangible outcomes in our solutions. In Clean Core for example, SAP HANA’s integration with SAP Build allows you to easily create AI-driven, context-aware applications while keeping workloads separate from your ERP system. In our lecture DA102, Henkel will share how they optimized trade promotions with HANA Cloud ML capabilities.

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar):

Kicking your journey off we recommend watching the Track Intro “DA100 – Data in the Age of AI” with Tobias and Britta. We will summarize our complete lecture portfolio, talk about recent deliveries and investment areas for the future and introduce some deep-dive topics.

  1. DA103: Generative AI–driven business transformation enabled by SAP HANA Cloud: Hear from Markus and Christoph how SAP HANA supports AI initiatives.
  2. DA106: Integration of SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere solutions: Listen to Maximilian and see how we bring two solutions more and more together - #BetterTogether!
  3. DA110: How to treat data as a product: Join Suva and Florian to learn about how Data Products together with SAP Datasphere will foster agility.
  4. DA200: Best features of SAP BW in SAP Datasphere: Learn from Dominik and Klaus-Peter about features you know from SAP BW that have made their way natively into SAP Datasphere.

 Please do not forget to register for our two Jumpstart sessions DA180 and DA181.

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024?

  • Comfortable desk and chair: As TechEd is 100% virtual this year, it is important to feel comfortable - feel free to stay in your pajamas 😉!
  • Be open and curious to explore the sessions we have prepared for you!
  • Agenda Builder - as you will find many interesting sessions, this will help you maximize your time. But don’t worry, if you miss sessions, look for the recording afterwards.

Check out our Session Catalog and register for the lectures of your interest!

See you at Teched &  

Britta & Eduardo

entry.title: SAP Discovery Center at SAP TechEd 2024: Sessions, Resources, and Highlights
entry.content:
Planning, preparations, and excitement are all in full swing for SAP TechEd 2024! The event will kick-off virtually before heading out on a global tour, making attendance easier than ever. The session catalog is filled with hot topics and content experts, eager to share the latest innovations and news coming out of SAP. If you haven’t done so already, register for SAP TechEd 2024.

Whether you are new to SAP Business Technology Platform or a returning veteran, SAP Discovery Center is your go-to resource. From detailed use cases and service information to step-by-step missions and reference architectures, the Discovery Center helps you realize the full potential of SAP BTP, accelerating product adoption and driving informed decisions. Haven’t explored it yet? Now’s the perfect time! Go to SAP Discovery Center.

TechEd Sessions Featuring SAP Discovery Center

For this year’s SAP TechEd, Discovery Center will be featured, including deep dive demos, in the following sessions:

Session XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center
Explore the latest enhancements in speed of project implementation and personalization in SAP Discovery Center. See the powerful advantages of guided implementation projects and reference architectures curated by SAP. Learn how to effectively use the service catalog in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), the extensive list of real-world use cases, and your control center. Whether a new or experienced user, you’ll get the knowledge and tools to accelerate your SAP BTP journey.
Speakers: Nis Boy Naeve and Marco Geldmacher

Session XP105 | Start your journey with SAP BTP and confidently accelerate adoption
Are you new to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) or seeking guidance on best practices and architectural decisions? Take a look at the powerful advantages of guided implementation projects curated by SAP experts and partners with SAP Discovery Center. Discover how the SAP BTP Guidance Framework assists you with decision guides, reference architectures, and best practices to design, develop, and operate enterprise-grade applications on SAP BTP.
Speakers: Konstantin Heine and Cecily Sorenson

Session XP204 | Modernize your technology landscape with SAP BTP and SAP LeanIX solutions
Discover how SAP LeanIX solutions can transform your as-is technology landscape leveraging SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). Learn how to get instant visibility in your as-is SAP landscape using automated application discovery. Model your SAP BTP landscape with best practices, define your to-be technology landscape with the help of reference content based on SAP BTP Guidance Framework, and manage your technology transformation, governance compliance, and risks using report templates.
Speakers: Katrin von Ahsen and Jens Rieskamp

Don’t Miss the SAP TechEd Learning Zone

It wouldn’t be TechEd without a core emphasis on learning and upskilling opportunities. This year’s TechEd Learning Zone features tailored learning experiences for each content track. Within each track you will also find reference to six key use cases. These use cases have been hand-selected for their topic relevance and quality guidance.

More About SAP BTP Use Cases

SAP BTP use cases consist of customer-proven, well-documented missions for an easy implementation of SAP BTP. They support you in solving problems in a repeatable manner to achieve the desired business outcome faster. To learn more about what SAP BTP looks like in real life and how it drives business innovation and success, explore the featured use cases.

What’s New in SAP Discovery Center

Exciting new features are coming to SAP Discovery Center! Stay tuned by subscribing to this blog post or following me on LinkedIn to be one of the first to know—you won’t want to miss it!

Update October 7: The latest innovations have just been unveiled! Take a look: SAP Discovery Center Innovations: AI Features, Terraform Automation, and More.

entry.title: Real Talk with Suna Singh | Track Lead for Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Hello, SAP Community members 👋

I am Suna Singh, an Operations Expert at Cloud ERP and one of my passions is with SAP TechEd. Beyond that, I like travelling around the world to meet new people and cultures and embrace new adventures. I also love skiing and reading.   

In the track “Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP” you will explore how SAP S/4HANA Cloud can enhance your digital transformation by supporting your business needs from mission-critical operations to business-model innovation. Whether adopting your first ERP or migrating from an existing one to the cloud, you can gain a complete, modular solution with the AI and analytics capabilities through exploring our GROW with SAP and RISE with SAP solutions. Learn ways to extend and embed SAP S/4HANA Cloud and use the power of AI and automation.

What inspired you to specialize in this track? 

To support your digital transformation, we provide a broad range of topics such as transitioning paths to SAP S/4HANA Cloud including tools and services, extensibility, integration with SAP Business Technology Platform, architecture, clean core, AI, user experience, security, and many more. 

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

Our experts will not only talk about the latest innovations, they will also showcase various demos and share best practices with you.  

In the Jump-Start sessions - DT180 Integrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP IBP using SAP Integration Suite and DT280 Build clean core extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud - you will get the opportunity to gain hands-on experience and complete the exercises at your own pace.  

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar) 

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future: Explore the future of cloud ERP, focusing on the latest innovations and strategies driving digital transformation. 
  • DT101 | Choosing the right path for your business to SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Find out about the important factors, explore the right option for your organization, and get insights into SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408. 
  • DT102 | Tools and services to make your transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud easier: Discover tools and strategies for your transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud with the RISE with SAP solution. 
  • DT201 | Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Learn about key user and development tools in the ABAP Cloud development model and how customers and partners can manage their on-stack extensions to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.  
  • DT103 | SAP Business AI in cloud ERP: Understand how your business processes are supported by cloud ERP solutions from SAP with the product portfolio enhanced with AI and other intelligent technologies, including traditional machine learning, large language models, generative AI, and situation handling.  

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024? 

  • Agenda Builder: Build your own agenda in order not to miss any session you are interested in. 
  • Explore: Explore our session offering, learn from industry leaders, and discover best practices from SAP experts and partners. 
  • Inspiration: Join us for a high-energy, interactive virtual event experience and get inspired. 

Want to learn more about the track Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP? Explore the  TechEd 2024 session catalog and ensure your favorite track sessions are on your list.  

Don’t miss the event and and sign up today!

entry.title: SAP Fiori development newsletter September 2024 (issue #30)
entry.content:
September marks the start of autumn, which means back to school for our kids and conference season for us. Both are focused on learning new things, which is so important in our industry where technology changes so quickly.

Some events to look for include Devtoberfest, SAP TechEd, and the next SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg.

If you want to meet with fellow developers and the SAP engineers who build our professional development tools, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori elements and SAP Fiori tools roundtable group. Our next meeting is Wednesday, October 9, 2024. You can also email me if you would like an invitation.

If you prefer to receive this in your inbox, please subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter.

Development News

How to create an SAP Fiori elements application from within ADT

This integration helps bridge the gaps between the different development environments that are currently required to build an SAP Fiori elements app and deploy it on an ABAP stack.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-create-a-sap-fiori-elements-application-from-within-adt/ba-p/13797232

Side Effects in SAP Fiori elements apps: triggering actions and updating data in your unmanaged RAP application Side Effects refer to system behaviors where a user’s action, such as modifying a field or performing a specific task, triggers changes in other parts of the user interface. For instance, updating a field might automatically adjust related fields or data sections. These changes are crucial for maintaining data consistency and ensuring that the user interface reflects the latest information.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/side-effects-in-fiori-triggering-actions-and-updating-data-in-your/ba-p/13851571

How to mass upload from a spreadsheet file into an SAP Fiori app in SAP BTP ABAP Environment

All SAP Fiori elements apps provide a download to Excel as a standard feature. However, only a few apps support uploading from a spreadsheet. In this series, you will see how to upload records from a spreadsheet file.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-mass-upload-from-a-spreadsheet-file-into-an-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-btp/ba-p/13785946 (part 1)
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-mass-upload-from-a-spreadsheet-file-into-an-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-btp/ba-p/13800792 (part 2)

Smart and flexible: sap.ui.mdc library is released for productive use in SAPUI5 1.124

sap.ui.mdc helps to build enterprise-ready smart controls or applications, no matter which back-end technology and data protocol you are using. It offers a flexible and comprehensive reuse approach with implementable delegates for the sap.ui.mdc controls. Using specific protocols like FHIR or even a plain REST service is now possible without having to start from scratch.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/smart-and-flexible-sap-ui-mdc-released-for-productive-use/ba-p/13793439

SAP BTP ABAP environment – release 2408

A summary of updates to ADT, CDS, RAP, and other innovations for ABAP developers.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-btp-abap-environment-release-2408/ba-p/13801154

Events

Devtoberfest starts September 23

Devtoberfest aims to capture the experience of live pre-conference events, like SAP InnoJam of years’ past. It officially kicks off the SAP TechEd season. Devtoberfest takes some of the best aspects of the traditional hackathon type event, such as learning by building something, but opens the experience to the entire community and spreads across multiple weeks to better accommodate everyone’s busy schedules.
https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-welcome/ba-p/9358

SAP TechEd runs October 8-9

User experience and app dev sessions at SAP TechEd 2024

For 2024, SAP TechEd is virtual once again, with lots of great sessions grouped into six content tracks. If you prefer in-person learning, SAP TechEd on Tour consists of companion events around the world in collaboration with regional user groups (ASUG, SAUG, UKISUG, and DSAG).

Here are some sessions on UX and application development.
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/user-experience-and-app-dev-sessions-at-sap-teched-2024-virtual/ba-p/13864322

SAPUI5 at SAP TechEd: tailor your UI with SAPUI5 in SAP Build Code

Discover the latest on SAPUI5 development in SAP Build Code. Learn how to realize clean core extensibility with new capabilities and how you can future-proof your app with new tools and best practices. Understand why SAP Web Components are strategic and how you can benefit from using them.
See AD101 in the session catalog

Full TechEd session catalog

Content tracks – we recommend you check the application development and automation tracks

SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg on November 19

After our successful events in Dublin and Chicago, we are bringing SAP Fiori Innovation Day back to Europe. The next one will be in Heidelberg on November 19. By attending this event, you will learn:

  • SAP’s UX strategy and how it is evolving
  • Recent UX innovations that fulfill the promise of the intelligent, collaborative enterprise
  • Making SAP Fiori work for you – finding the apps you need and adapting them to your business requirements
  • Building apps using the latest tools and technologies – the same ones SAP uses to build apps for SAP S/4HANA
  • How to move your organization forward, including how to take advantage of AI for developing applications

Register today:
https://events.sap.com/eur/sap-fiori-innovation-day-heidelberg-2024/en/home

Summary of SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago

If you want to gain a sense of what we do in SAP Fiori Innovation Day and what you might learn, you can read the summary of our event in Chicago.
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-innovation-day-in-chicago-sap-fiori-fundamentals-ux/ba-p/13797787

Tutorials

openSAP content has moved to the learning.sap.com platform

Developing and Extending SAP Fiori Elements Apps
https://learning.sap.com/courses/developing-and-extending-sap-fiori-elements-apps

Developing an SAP Fiori Elements App Based on a CAP OData V4 Service
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-a-cap-odata-v4-service

Getting Started with Creating an SAP Fiori Elements App Based on an OData V4 RAP Service

This new learning journey guides you through building SAP Fiori elements applications from scratch using a RAP OData V4 service.
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/getting-started-with-creating-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-an-odata-v4-rap-service

Adapting the UI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Using SAP Screen Personas

https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas

Tutorial: Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 applications

To extend SAPUI5 applications, SAPUI5 Flexibility offers capabilities not only for developers, but also for key users and end users using simple visual / WYSIWYG tools.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/new-tutorial-mission-key-user-adaptation-for-sapui5-applications/ba-p/13639694

Tutorial: SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio are not new. What is new, is the support for SAP S/HANA Cloud. This enables developers to enhance SAPUI5 applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with features that were previously only for on-premise customers. See this blog post linking to an extensive, end-to-end tutorial:
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/extending-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-using-sapui5-adaptation-projects/ba-p/13607813

Back issues from the past year

SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2024 (issue #29)

SAP FIori development newsletter #28 (May 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #27 (March 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #26 (January 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #25 (November 2023)

entry.title: Plan your agenda for TechEd Virtual
entry.content:
It’s time to start planning your agenda for SAP TechEd Virtual! Head over to the TechEd Virtual dashboard to begin organizing your sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/dashboard/page/dashboard

To help you get started, here are a few recommendations that might be useful:

Keynote & Developer Keynote

By default, the Keynote is automatically added to your list of scheduled sessions. This session offers a high-level overview of the latest innovations and cool new developments from SAP. It also provides valuable insights into SAP's future strategy.

However, don’t overlook the Developer Keynote! This is another essential session to include in your TechEd Virtual agenda. Unlike the main keynote, the Developer Keynote is all about diving deep into the technical aspects. If you're keen on hands-on technical insights and want a closer look at the latest tools and developments, this session is a must!

 

Prioritize your interests

Next, start by applying a filter to narrow down the list of sessions to those that align with your interests. This should be your priority, focusing on sessions that offer updates and new insights in areas you’re passionate about or working on. Once you’ve locked in these key sessions, you can fill any gaps in your schedule with other sessions that catch your eye or topics you’d like to explore further.

Personally, I selected the following tracks as a filter:

 

Maximize your schedule

Once you’ve selected all the sessions that match your interests, it’s time to fill any gaps in your schedule. Head to your dashboard and identify where those gaps are in your list of scheduled sessions. Then, browse the session catalog to find additional sessions that fit those time slots. Choose the ones that seem most interesting or relevant to you.

For example, I had a gap between 10:30 am and 11:00 am on the first day:

Remove all the filters:

And look at the sessions available at that time. I filled this gap with the Keynote recap + Community welcome:

 

Plan backup sessions

It's a good idea to plan backup sessions in case your scheduled session doesn't meet your expectations. By having an alternative ready, you can easily switch if needed. While scheduling a backup session at the same time will create a conflict, you can simply keep the one you prefer and mark the backup as a favorite. This allows you to quickly jump to it if needed, or catch the replay afterward.

Many sessions will focus on Joule, both as an AI assistant for our business and as a powerful tool for developers. I’m really excited to explore all the new innovations Joule has to offer. It’s definitely something to keep an eye on during SAP TechEd Virtual! (though it will be hard to miss! 😊)

Be sure to join the SAP TechEd community group to connect and network with fellow community members. Also, don’t forget to sign up for Devtoberfest for even more learning and fun!

 

If you’re feeling overwhelmed by virtual sessions, consider attending SAPInsider EMEA in Copenhagen, which is partnering with TechEd on Tour: https://reg.eventmobi.com/emea-2024. Tom Cenens and I will be presenting a session on BTP titled “Key Strategies for Effective SAP BTP Administration and Governance.” It’s a great opportunity to connect in person and dive deep into BTP strategies!

entry.title: SAP Developer News, September 19th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/19/sap-developer-news-september-19th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

Devtoberfest Launch

  • Devtoberfest Community Home: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Events Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Gameboard: https://devrel-tools-prod-scn-badges-srv.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/devtoberfestContest/scnId.Here
  • Devtoberfest Playlist on YouTube: https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d&si=R26dQIns-1e97fU0

SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/aug24

Devtoberfest Crossword Puzzle

  • https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-sap-crossword.html

Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

  •  https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-builders-blog-posts/transporting-content-in-sap-build-apps-sap-cloud-transport-management/ba-p/13737478

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 Devtoberfest Launch
2:09 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model August 2024 Release
7:56 Devtoberfest Puzzle
9:06 Transporting content in SAP Build Apps

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 19th, 2024.

[Sheena] Hello SAP community. The much-awaited Detoberfest 2024 is here, running from September 23rd to October 25th. Devtoberfest helps to prepare for SAP TechEd and offers a mix of educational sessions, hands-on developer tools, and fun activities like Petoberfest, Bugtoberfest, and Spooktoberfest. It is a point-based contest, and the Devtoberfest animated contest game board is already live. You can earn points by attending sessions, completing developer tutorials linked to weekly topics or engaging in activities that benefit the SAP Developer Community.

The grand prize is a retreat at an SAP TechEd Roadshow stop in 2025. Exciting, right? To start participating, join the Devtoberfest group after reviewing the legal terms and conditions posted there.

We will kickstart Devtoberfest on Monday, September 23rd, which is the ABAP/CAP Day. Week 1, we are bringing to you many exciting sessions in ABAP, mainly on clean core extensibility, building partner products on SAP BTP, major updates in embedded analytics, and data integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS external entities. Week 1 also covers an exciting session on CAP, it's time to TypeScript (CAP edition).

On tooling Tuesdays, we'll focus on SAP Build Process Automation, Build Code and Build Apps.

Integration Wednesdays will have sessions on FlashPy, CPI Helper, and CPI Lint.

MAD Thursdays will focus on machine learning, AI, and data with sessions on SAP Generative AI Hub, SAP Datasphere, etc.

Frontend Fridays will have sessions on UI5 Freestyle and SAP Fiori tools.

Don't forget to participate in the fun Friday event Petoberfest by posting pictures with your pets at your workstation. So join us in this fun ride of learning and enjoy. Good morning.

[DJ] The latest release of CAP August of 2024, in fact, is out. And with it, there are improvements and fixes to the latest major release 8, which came out earlier this year, in the form of CAP Node.js 8.2.1 and CAP Java 3.2.0. Let me take this opportunity on my walk this morning to go through some of those highlights.

So first up, when the latest major release of CAP came out earlier this year, the new database services went GA, including the database service for SAP HANA. One of the benefits of this new database service for the Node.js runtime is that HANA native associations were no longer required to represent associations at the CDS level. This was great, mostly because it brought longer deploy times when we used SAP HANA Native Associations, plus there was the chance of indirect dependencies also being created. And if you're wondering about the Java runtime, the Java runtime hasn't used native SAP HANA Associations for a while. Anyway, at the time, the release notes advised us to switch off the HANA native associations using configuration. With this August 2024 release, that switch is now automatic. When you start a new project and do, for example, CDS add HANA, you get a switch in the configuration that turns these native associations off by default.

In the area of the CDS language and compiler, you can now use CDS deploy dash dash script to generate a SQL script as a starting point or manual schema migration. Note that in contrast to the existing dash dash dry option, it accepts and produces DDL statements that could lead to data loss. So are A, not supported for automatic schema migration and B, contains relevant warnings in the right places.

In the Node.js runtime, there are also some important changes. One of which is that linked definitions, the class that is used to represent internally structures of services and more, now no longer have the convenience methods such as for each map filter and so on that you would normally expect to have to process the linked definitions as arrays. Because basically linked definitions are not arrays, they're more than that. So these methods have been removed. In my opinion, this is great because it reminds us that we have to treat the data as it is. And so one thing to do is maybe convert the data to arrays using the rest parameter inside, for example, an array, literal array constructor.

Another improvement is that you can now programmatically, i.e. dynamically at runtime, control and activate the CDS debug output at runtime. This means you can turn on debug for cat modules as you go.

In the Java runtime, there's now beta level support for recursive hierarchies with Odata v4 in SAP HANA. Recursive hierarchy is an experimental term in the OData hierarchy vocabulary and is used, for example, with SAP Fiori tree tables. Treetables. You can also now define the name of the Java interfaces generated for CDS entities and structured types using a new annotation. In the multi-tenancy area, activating extensions using the SAP extensibility service can now be done asynchronously. You achieve this by sending a prefer header in the HTTP request with the value of respond async. This in my opinion is a really neat way of coordinating this using the RFC 7240 standard authored by an old acquaintance of mine.

Well I'm almost home but there's just time to tell you about improvements in the tools area. So first for all you IntelliJ users out there the SAP CDS language support plugin is now available on the JetBrains marketplace which means it's directly installable now. Great! For all you TypeScript users there's a new CLI command "CDS TSX", which in contrast to the existing CDS TS command uses a different faster engine. This feature is in beta still at the moment.

So I'm back home. There's one more thing to tell you about and that is if you want to use the SAP Cloud Logging Service instead of the SAP Application Logging Service, you can do that. And there's an option to use telemetry as well, including an option to use Dynatrace. What's not to like? Anyway, that's it for this August 2024 release. See See you next time.

[Shrini and Daniel] Hello, everybody. I'm at Tbilisi, Georgia with Daniel at the EPUB office doing SAP Build CodeJam. It's really great to be here. Yeah, it's been a lot of fun and because it's only a couple days before Devtoberfest, we wanted to tell you something about the contest. So the contest you can earn enough points to get a trip to TechEd perhaps next year and one of the ways to get points is by doing the traditional SAP crossword. So there are topics about SAP Build and JavaScript and even a reference to Analytics Cloud and it would help to know something about Sabrina Carpenter. Can anybody do the Cross Word? Yes, definitely. Of course, it helps to know a little bit about SAP. There are 33 questions. Fairly easy, a few tougher ones, but I think everybody can do it, and it might even be worth a couple thousand points. And it's already published, so even before the Devtoberfest starts, you can accumulate points. So that's pretty good. That's good to know. Yeah. Good luck to everybody.

[Rekha] Hello, everybody. We all know the importance of having development, test, and productive environments during the application development. SAP has recently introduced project versioning for SAP Build apps, web, and mobile projects. This allows you to transport the released project versions between development, testing, and productive subaccounts on SAP BTP. A transport setup of Cloud Transport Management Service, CTMS, Content Agent Service, CAS, and BTP destinations are required to start transporting SAP Build Apps projects between the three tiered dev test prod landscapes. There are four types of subaccounts or tenants involved in the scenario. A source subaccount, this is where the actual development takes place. A target, under this we have two subaccounts, testing, and production, and a central sub-account to administer the content transport between the source and target sub-accounts. In order to enable the transport, you will have to enable the Cloud Foundry, subscribe to SAP Build apps and content agent services, create an instance of content agent, and generate a service key on the source and target sub-accounts.

On the central sub-account, you need to subscribe to Cloud Transport Management service, create an instance of Cloud Transport Management, and generate a service key. The MBS role collection has to be enabled in BTP cockpit to authorize the users for performing the transport activities. SAP Burn Lobby configuration has to be done in the control tower in order to set the sub-account type to decide if it's a dev, test, or prod subaccount. Then BTP destinations has to be set from source to central subaccount, and then from central to target, that is the test and prod subaccounts. Finally, set up the transport landscape using the Cloud Transport Management user interface. For more details on this topic, do refer to this interesting blog post by SAP Build expert Mari Heary. Link is provided in the description box. Enjoy learning and thank you.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, September 20th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, September 23rd.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the first week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt will be posted here in week 3. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  We are kicking things off there with the ever-popular Petoberfest. 

List of activities for contest week 1:
